fiuAuthority.name: COAF (Conselho de Controle de Atividades Financeiras)

reportingWindow.source: Brazilian AML Law No. 9613/1998 as amended

requirements: Prior formal investigation must exist before RIF shared externally

governingInstruments.name: Brazilian AML Law No. 9,613/1998 (as amended)
platformImplications: Mandatory AML programs for all regulated entities
Suspicious activity must be reported to COAF within 24 hours
STR/RIF existence is confidential
Tipping-off equivalent prohibition applies

governingInstruments.name: BCB Resolution establishing COAF reporting obligations (various, as amended to 2025)
platformImplications: Financial institutions report to COAF via SISCOAF
Risk-based approach to suspicious transaction identification
Threshold and non-threshold reporting both required

governingInstruments.name: BCB Resolution No. 506/2025 (Pix Cybersecurity and Fraud Notifications)
platformImplications: Fraud notifications mandatory: CPF/CNPJ must be flagged when linked to fraudulent Pix transactions
Participants must reject Pix key registrations for users associated with fraud notifications
All Pix transactions involving flagged users must be blocked (except refunds)
Fraud notification system creates a domestic signal-sharing mechanism via DICT
Platform may receive and process DICT fraud notification indicators

governingInstruments.name: BCB Resolution No. 493/2025 (Pix MED Enhancement)
platformImplications: Immediate and cascade fund blocking upon fraud notification
Payer institution has autonomous debit and return authority
DICT operational manual specifies tracking mechanisms and partial block capabilities
Platform signals should include MED case reference where applicable

governingInstruments.name: BCB Resolution No. 429/2024 (Pix Participation Restriction)
platformImplications: Only BCB-authorised institutions may participate in Pix
Platform must verify BCB authorisation status of Brazilian participant institutions

governingInstruments.name: BCB Normative No. 491/2024 (Pix Device Limits)
platformImplications: First-use device signals must include device registration status
Unregistered device flag: transaction limit R$200 single / R$1,000 daily
Device risk tier must be propagated in signal metadata

governingInstruments.name: BCB Resolution (September 11, 2025) - Mandatory Fraud Transaction Rejection
platformImplications: Institutions must reject transactions to accounts reasonably suspected of fraud
Must use all available information including public/private databases
Notification to customer of blocked transactions required
Targeted at money mule accounts and organised crime group accounts

governingInstruments.name: BCB Resolution No. 342/2023 (Pix Data Incident Notification)
platformImplications: Pix participants must notify account holders of personal data breaches
Platform breach notification obligations triggered if DICT data exposed
72-hour notification window expected (LGPD Art. 48)

governingInstruments.name: CMN Resolution No. 5,274/2025 and BCB Resolution No. 538/2025 (Additional Cybersecurity Requirements)
platformImplications: Strengthened data communication infrastructure security for SFN and SPB
End-to-end encryption mandatory in transit for all financial data
Platform must satisfy BCB cybersecurity standards for Brazilian participants

governingInstruments.name: Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD), Law No. 13,709/2018
platformImplications: Data minimisation principle (Art. 6(III)): only fields necessary for fraud typology
Purpose limitation: signals may only be used for AML/fraud prevention
International transfers require ANPD SCC, BCR, or adequacy decision
No anonymised data transfer restriction (Art. 12)

governingInstruments.name: ANPD Resolution CD/ANPD No. 19/2024 (International Data Transfer Rules)
platformImplications: ANPD-approved SCCs must be in place before any personal data leaves Brazil
No adequacy decisions issued as of May 2026
BCRs permitted for intra-group transfers
Specific contractual clauses permitted on case-by-case ANPD approval
Platform operating as data processor must execute ANPD SCCs with Brazilian FI participants

governingInstruments.name: STF Preliminary Injunction on COAF RIF Sharing (March 27, 2026)
platformImplications: COAF financial intelligence reports cannot be generically requested or shared
Prior formal investigation required
Judicial or parliamentary oversight required for RIF disclosure
Platform must not serve as mechanism for circumventing STF restrictions
Signals derived from COAF data must be separately reviewed for STF compliance

tippingOffFilter.legalBasis: AML Law No. 9613/1998 / STF March 2026 injunction

dataProtection.framework: LGPD Law No. 13,709/2018 / ANPD Resolution 19/2024 / BCB Cybersecurity Resolutions

indirectIdentifiers.note: CPF/CNPJ must be HMAC-hashed before any cross-border transmission; plaintext prohibited

pseudonymisationStandard.lgpdBasis: LGPD Art. 12 anonymisation threshold not met by unsalted hashing
